_risk_level_doc: Audit-only task. No code changes. Risk is in misreporting a regression as intact, not in system modification.

requirement.original: Audit three server infrastructure files that were modified during previous sessions and verify their features are intact. Produce a written audit report as the deliverable — this is an investigation task, not a code change task. Also answer: whether the 6-hour disk-usage-monitor cron job is fully restored and low-overhead.
requirement.clarified: Read and verify the following files against their stated original specifications: daily-trade-scheduler.{service,timer}, auto-safe-swap-drain.{service,timer,sh}, happy-daemon-post-start.sh, and disk-usage-monitor.sh + crontab. Report findings. No changes.
requirement.what: Infrastructure audit report — per-component INTACT/REGRESSION verdict
requirement.why: Prior dev sessions modified these files and may have introduced regressions; user wants confirmation before proceeding with further work

included: PID validation and restore spawn in happy-daemon-post-start.sh
disk-usage-monitor cron presence, executable status, and overhead assessment
excluded: Network behavior of the triggered scripts

success_criteria: Each of the four file groups has a clear INTACT or REGRESSION verdict with file:line evidence
User question about disk-usage-monitor is definitively answered
constraints: Read-only investigation — no file edits, no systemctl restarts, no crontab modifications

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User cannot confirm whether prior dev session modifications degraded the feature set of infrastructure files
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Prior sessions modified systemd units and shell scripts as part of broader daemon tracking work; no audit was produced at the time
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — audit task, not a regression fix
observations: daily-trade-scheduler.timer: OnCalendar=Mon..Fri *-*-* 09:03:00 America/New_York (observed: file read line 5)
daily-trade-scheduler.timer: next fire Mon 2026-05-25 13:03:00 UTC = 09:03 ET (observed: systemctl list-timers)
daily-trade-scheduler.service: DAILY_TRADE_PROMPT=/daily-trade --auto --codex (observed: line 27; --codex added 2026-05-18 per inline comment — deliberate, not regression)
auto-safe-swap-drain.timer: OnBootSec=10min + OnUnitActiveSec=5min (observed: file read lines 5-6)
auto-safe-swap-drain.timer: enabled + active, last fired 2min 31s ago (observed: systemctl list-timers + is-enabled/is-active)
auto-safe-swap-drain.sh: MAX_LOAD1_INT=6, check is [[ LOAD1_INT -ge 6 ]] (observed: lines 7, 39-42)
happy-daemon-post-start.sh: polls up to 15 attempts, validates PID via /proc cmdline + env (observed: lines 20-61)
happy-daemon-post-start.sh: nohup bash -c sleep 20 && /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh restore ... & (observed: line 53)
disk-usage-monitor.sh: pure read-only monitoring, log append + 500-line rotation, no network, no subprocess chains (observed: full file read lines 1-92)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (all claims are observed from live file reads and systemctl queries)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: All files read directly from disk. Timer states queried via systemctl. Crontab queried via crontab -l. No inference — all findings are direct observations.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: All seven files confirmed at their declared paths

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — audit task, no regression identified

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: daily-trade-scheduler.service and .timer — originally fires Mon-Fri 09:03 ET to run /root/bin/daily-trade-scheduler.py
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: timer OnCalendar = Mon..Fri *-*-* 09:03:00 America/New_York, ExecStart = /usr/bin/python3 /root/bin/daily-trade-scheduler.py, timer enabled and active

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: auto-safe-swap-drain.service and .timer — originally fires every 5 minutes, conservative pre-flight checks: swap <= 8GB, mem available >= 8GB, load1 < 6, PSI < 1.00
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: OnUnitActiveSec=5min, MAX_AUTO_SWAP_USED_KB=8388608, MIN_MEM_AVAILABLE_KB=8388608, MAX_LOAD1_INT=6, MAX_MEM_PSI_AVG10=1.00, timer enabled and active

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: happy-daemon-post-start.sh — originally validates daemon PID and spawns restore in background with 20s delay
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: pid_matches_home() validates /proc cmdline and env, nohup sleep 20 && happy-session-recovery.sh restore spawned in background

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: whether the 6-hour disk-usage-monitor cron job (/root/bin/disk-usage-monitor.sh, runs every 6 hours) is fully restored and low-overhead
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: crontab entry '0 */6 * * *' for disk-usage-monitor.sh present, script is executable, script has no heavy operations

notes: --codex added to DAILY_TRADE_PROMPT on 2026-05-18 (documented in service file comment line 26; deliberate enhancement, not regression)

happy-daemon-post-start.sh uses global (non-scoped) restore — documented in inline comment lines 48-52 as intentional design decision to avoid race condition where scoped restore leaves other homes un-recovered

setup.reason: non-UI -- config; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: User states files were modified in prior sessions and may have regressions

measured.value: All files read directly from disk at audit time; systemctl and crontab queried live
measured.location: Multiple — see observations[] array in root_cause_analysis

expected.value: Schedules and thresholds as specified in user requirement document

evidence.gap: None — all expected values were found at their specified locations with correct values

development_approach.strategy: No development required — audit-only task. All components verified intact.
development_approach.validation_approach: Audit report is the deliverable; user reviews the per-component verdicts

research_findings.reason: Audit of existing infrastructure files — no best-practice research required; expected values are fully specified in user requirement
